codeReview.summary: Implementation addresses the targeted assertions for Shift+Click range anchor handling, OrgChart lasso/store synchronization, and approvals UX/tooltip copy updates. I found one non-blocking regression risk in OrgChart drag-reset flows where selection state can be unintentionally cleared.

issues.description: `handleNodeDragStop`/cancel paths call `setNodes(nodesWithLayout)` where `nodesWithLayout` intentionally omits `selected`. This can clear React Flow selection on invalid drop/cancel (and may clear store selection via `onSelectionChange`), creating an unexpected selection reset regression after drag flows. Preserve previous `selected` flags (or reapply `selectedIds`) when resetting positions.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `implementation-worker` skill requires agent-browser manual verification for UI changes, but this UI-heavy feature appears to have completed without agent-browser usage while still marked as followed procedure.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton for session 9bd13c77-1066-4973-83f3-b138587db5fc shows no `agent-browser` invocation/tooling; handoff verification lists command-line checks only and no interactiveChecks, yet `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` is true.

summary: Pass with one non-blocking regression risk: the core targeted fixes are present and well-covered by tests, but OrgChart drag reset logic may unintentionally clear selection state. Also noted a skill-procedure enforcement gap around required manual UI verification.
